WorkForge Supply Co.
Performance

Brand overhaul drove 2.8x revenue in one product cycle

Repositioned a commoditised product brand with a narrative-first content system and paid social engine. New creative direction and full-funnel strategy compounded into a breakout quarter.

0.0xRevenue Growth
0.0xROAS
0%Repeat Purchase Rate
DTC E-commerce2025
The Challenge

What was broken — and why it mattered.

“A strong product was losing ground to cheaper competitors — the brand had no differentiated positioning and paid channels were burning budget on bottom-of-funnel only.”

The product was genuinely good — but the brand was invisible at the top of funnel and over-indexed on discount-driven retargeting at the bottom. Paid social was averaging a 1.4x ROAS on prospecting audiences. Repeat purchase rates were low because customers had no emotional connection to the brand — they had just found a deal.

The Approach

How we built the solution.

Service pillars engaged
PerformanceBrand Presence

“Built a narrative-led brand architecture and full-funnel paid social system with creative direction, audience segmentation, and a retention email layer.”

We rebuilt brand architecture around a clear identity: rugged utility, maker culture, and the pride of lasting tools. New creative direction established a visual language and narrative voice, then fed into a full-funnel paid social system — awareness creative to build affinity, mid-funnel story sequences to educate, and conversion content that didn't rely on discounts. An email retention layer locked in repeat buyers.

The Outcomes

Results that held.

The brand effect compounded into the numbers: ROAS on prospecting moved from 1.4x to 4.1x as creative quality lifted click-through, and repeat purchase rate grew to 68% as customers developed brand loyalty rather than deal loyalty. Revenue grew 2.8x over one product cycle — a result that held into the following season.

0.0xRevenue Growth
0.0xROAS
0%Repeat Purchase Rate

How we delivered this.

Performance

Key Insight

The brand effect compounded into the numbers: ROAS on prospecting moved from 1.4x to 4.1x as creative quality lifted click-through, and repeat purchase rate grew to 68% as customers developed brand loyalty rather than deal loyalty. Revenue grew 2.8x over one product cycle — a result that held into the following season.
